Land Rover Freelander Td4 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Freelander Td4 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M47R engine typically costs £1,800–£3,200 supply only, with fitting adding £900–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Freelander.

mainEntity.name: Is the Freelander Td4 (BMW M47R) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M47R is widely regarded as one of the most reliable engines fitted to the Freelander. It's a BMW-sourced, chain-driven diesel with a robust design. The main watchpoints are the Bosch VP44 injection pump and the turbocharger — both are serviceable and well-understood.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Freelander Td4 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injection pump wear (Bosch VP44), turbocharger failure (oil starvation), and PCV breather blockage. The engine itself is durable and capable of high mileage with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Freelander Td4 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An FL1 Freelander is a £1,000–£5,000 vehicle — a £2,500 recon is 50–250% of the car's value. For lower-value examples (sub-£2,500), replacement often exceeds our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold. Evaluate carefully — a well-maintained, low-mileage example may justify replacement; a tired one may not.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Freelander Td4 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The Td4 engine code is BMW M47R — a 2.0-litre 16-valve turbo-diesel. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the injection pump failure on a Td4?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — regular fuel filter changes (every 20,000 miles) and using high-quality diesel can extend the VP44 pump's life. If you experience poor starting or loss of power, have the pump tested immediately — rebuilding it is far cheaper than replacing it.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current M47R era.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Freelander Td4 engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare Freelander Td4 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Injection Pump Failure (Bosch VP44)
problems.symptoms: Poor starting, rough idle, loss of power, engine stalling, inability to start when hot.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,200 (pump rebuild or replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The Bosch VP44 injection pump is the M47R's most significant weak point. It's an electronically-controlled rotary pump with a finite life — typically 100,000–180,000 miles. If you experience poor starting, loss of power, or stalling, have the pump checked immediately. A pump rebuild is far cheaper than the engine damage that can result from a complete failure.

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure (Oil Starvation)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, excessive smoke (blue/white), whistling or grinding noise from the turbo, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £600–£1,500 (turbo rebuild or replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before turbo disintegrates)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if turbo noise or smoke appears

problems.recommendation: The M47R's turbocharger is vulnerable to oil starvation, often due to blocked oil feed pipes or low oil pressure. Regular oil changes with the correct 5W30 BMW-spec oil are essential. If you hear a whistle or whine from the turbo, or see excessive smoke, have it inspected immediately.

problems.title: Crankcase Breather Blockage (PCV)
problems.symptoms: Increased oil consumption, blue smoke, oil leaks from the engine, poor running.
problems.recommendation: The M47R's crankcase breather (PCV) can become blocked over time, leading to excessive crankcase pressure, oil leaks, and increased oil consumption. The breather filter is a service item — replacing it every 40,000–60,000 miles is cheap and effective prevention.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Freelander Td4 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace or rebuild — cheaper than collateral damage
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall condition
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Freelander Td4? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented injection pump service history (or recent replacement)
* Turbocharger confirmed healthy with no smoke or noise
* PCV breather replaced within the last 40,000 miles
* Full service history with BMW-spec 5W30 oil
avoidIf: * Poor starting or rough idle with no injection pump history — walk away
* Blue or white smoke with no turbo history — may indicate turbo failure
* Engine oil pressure warning light — could indicate oil starvation
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive electrical gremlins

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M47R is one of the best engines fitted to the Freelander — a BMW-sourced, chain-driven diesel with a well-earned reputation for durability. The injection pump and turbo are the two watchpoints, but they're well-understood and serviceable. A well-maintained Td4 is a reliable, long-lived engine; a neglected one is a manageable repair bill, not a disaster.

cta.label: → Compare Freelander Td4 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Freelander Td4 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth starting; poor cold start can indicate injection pump wear
* Service History — including engine era (BMW M47R) and confirmed oil-change history
* Smoke Check — Blue or white smoke can indicate turbo issues; black smoke may indicate injector problems
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any whistle, whine, or power loss?
* Oil Pressure — Check the warning light; low pressure can cause turbo oil starvation
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
